---
title: "Appendix D: Production Checklist"
subtitle: "SPCS operations, monitoring, and sizing"
---

Use this checklist before promoting R + snowflakeR workloads from development to production.

---

## Workspace R: org CRE onboarding (platform / IT) {#sec-cre-org}

- [ ] **CRE v2** registered (`cre@sfnb_multilang_r` or org name) — [Bootstrap §CRE vs bootstrap](../../06_workspace_bootstrap/index.qmd#sec-cre-vs-bootstrap)
- [ ] Image built with `create_cre.sh` / CI; `snow custom-image validate` passed
- [ ] `CREATE OR REPLACE CUSTOM RUNTIME ENVIRONMENT` after each image push; digest communicated to users
- [ ] `GRANT USAGE ON CUSTOM RUNTIME ENVIRONMENT` to notebook roles
- [ ] Snowsight default or runbook points analysts to CRE (bootstrap only for sandbox)
- [ ] Separate **GPU** CRE + `BASE_IMAGE_TYPE = GPU` if using R/Python GPU packages
- [ ] Multilang public docs: [org operating model](https://github.com/Snowflake-Labs/snowflake-notebook-multilang/blob/main/docs/org_cre_operating_model.md)

---

## Configuration & packaging {#sec-config}

- [ ] Pre-built **R package tarballs** in bootstrap configs and **CRE build** profile ([Appendix B](../B_config_reference/index.qmd))
- [ ] `snowflaker_*.yaml` pins warehouse, database, schema, role — not session defaults
- [ ] **CRE image** built and validated for repeat workloads ([CRE chapter](../../09_custom_runtime_and_ml_jobs/index.qmd))
- [ ] `reticulate>=1.25` and compatible `rpy2` pinned in conda packages
- [ ] Python `snowflake-ml-python` version meets feature requirements (`sfr_check_environment()`)

---

## Security & network {#sec-security}

- [ ] **EAI** and network rules reviewed by security — hostname allowlists vs open egress
- [ ] No PATs or private keys committed to Git notebooks
- [ ] Service users use **key-pair JWT** with least-privilege roles
- [ ] Row access / masking policies applied on sensitive training data

---

## SPCS & parallel {#sec-spcs}

- [ ] Docker images built with **`--platform linux/amd64`**
- [ ] Compute pool **cycled** (suspend/resume) after image updates
- [ ] Stage I/O via **volume mounts** on workers — no SQL `GET`/`PUT` over REST API
- [ ] Worker images include all R package dependencies for fit **and** predict
- [ ] `STAGE_MOUNT` env var documented in worker runbooks

---

## Model Registry & serving {#sec-registry}

- [ ] Inference dependencies on **conda-forge** or custom inference image
- [ ] Column names and types match between training and scoring
- [ ] R models tested end-to-end: `sfr_log_model()` → deploy → `sfr_predict()` / batch
- [ ] CustomModel + rpy2 path validated in target SPCS image

---

## Monitoring & operations {#sec-ops}

- [ ] **Inference log** table populated with timestamp, prediction, optional label columns
- [ ] `sfr_add_monitor()` configured for critical models
- [ ] Alerting wired to drift/performance metrics (dashboard, email, ticketing)
- [ ] `SYSTEM$GET_SERVICE_LOGS` and event table access for SPCS fault diagnosis
- [ ] Warehouse and compute pool **auto-suspend** policies set

---

## Documentation & support {#sec-docs}

- [ ] Runbook for failed bootstrap (EAI toggle, admin SQL)
- [ ] Runbook for CRE attach / `%%R` unknown (wrong runtime, v1 vs v2, [Appendix G](../G_workspace_container_internals/index.qmd))
- [ ] Runbook for `exec format error` / job 395030 (wrong CPU arch)
- [ ] Ownership and escalation for community packages (snowflakeR, RSnowflake)
- [ ] Compliance sign-off for community-project disclaimer ([Introduction](../../00_introduction/index.qmd))

---

## Sizing (guidance) {#sec-sizing}

| Workload | Starting point |
|----------|----------------|
| Interactive notebook | XS–S warehouse; default compute pool |
| Feature materialization | Size warehouse to refresh SLA |
| Many-model SPCS | Pool with N workers × `CPU_X64_*` family; benchmark chunk size |
| Batch inference | Warehouse or `sfr_run_batch()` per registry docs |

Benchmark on representative data before locking pool/warehouse sizes.

---

## Disclaimer

Community packages — validate SLAs, support, and compliance with your organization before production cutover.
